title: Size Select: Compact Grid
pitch: The winning variant in the size select test. Where the dropdown hid sizes entirely, this approach kept them visible but made better use of the space: a clean 5-column CSS Grid that gives every swatch a consistent footprint regardless of how many sizes a product offers. It won because it improved the look and feel without taking anything away from the customer.
problem: The default size selector was visually inconsistent and consumed unpredictable amounts of vertical space depending on the number of sizes. The layout shifted between products, creating a jarring experience.
hypothesis: A compact, consistent grid layout would scan faster than the original, handle any number of sizes gracefully, and keep the CTA visible without hiding size options behind an interaction.
